methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Sabanto is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $20M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises nine companies across robotics and manufacturing—one public entity and eight private firms including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity—though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist to support a reliable median comparable approach.

Given data limitations, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised of $20M as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, the analysis implies a $40M valuation. This approach assumes Sabanto's funding trajectory aligns with standard venture progression patterns in the sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $34M to $46M, with a median of $40M, derived from a single applicable method. This narrow methodological foundation reflects a material constraint: the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier comparables to cross-validate through market multiples. Key uncertainties include unit economics, revenue traction, competitive positioning relative to named peers, and market conditions affecting venture step-up multiples in robotics. Additional financial or operational metrics would substantially improve confidence in this range.
